title: Kubernetes Purpose, Architecture, Reconciliation, and Environment Evidence · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Version Context Baseline
summary: A civic platform team must decide which availability problem Kubernetes can solve without mistaking orchestration for application correctness or infrastructure durability. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s version context baseline increment in cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation.
Distinguish Kubernetes core, distribution, cloud provider, CNI, CSI, CRI, Gateway implementation, add-on, and application responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Version Context Baseline · Theory K8s Version Context Baseline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Version Context Baseline contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Version Context Baseline · Theory K8s Version Context Baseline.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture client, server, api discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation. For Predict K8s Version Context Baseline · Theory K8s Version Context Baseline, keep K8s Version Context Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A theory team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Version Context Baseline condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes 1.36 Releases, Version Skew, and API Reference — Controls supported releases, patch lifecycle, component skew, API groups, schemas, feature states, deprecations, downloads, and upgrade compatibility.

For Predict K8s Version Context Baseline · Theory K8s Version Context Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: kubectl version alone proves the current context, server compatibility, and enabled APIs.

options.text: For predict k8s version context baseline, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof. This does not satisfy K8s Version Context Baseline for cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map.

Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
kubectl version alone proves the current context, server compatibility, and enabled APIs. That claim fails the predict k8s version context baseline evidence boundary for K8s Version Context Baseline.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to K8s Version Context Baseline while producing cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation. A defensible solution applies K8s Version Context Baseline, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-version-context-baseline — Capture client, server, API discovery, context, cluster identity, node, runtime, add-on, feature-state, and support-window evidence before mutation.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-architecture-reconciliation-evidence-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-architecture-reconciliation-evidence-2
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s architecture reconciliation evidence 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      request-path: kubectl -> api-server -> admission -> etcd
      controller-watch: desired-vs-observed
      node-path: scheduler -> kubelet -> runtime
      convergence: status-condition
    verify: kubectl version && kubectl cluster-info && kubectl get --raw /readyz?verbose
    failure: Reject client-version-only evidence, direct-etcd application access, or a claim that reconciliation guarantees application correctness.
    repair: Capture current context, server APIs, component authority, desired state, observed status, and the remaining distribution-specific boundary.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: kubectl version alone proves the current context, server compatibility, and enabled APIs. This fails the K8s Version Context Baseline contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-version-context-baseline-4dpfuo". Tailor its architecture-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-version-context-baseline-4dpfuo
  - competency: k8s-version-context-baseline
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: version context baseline
    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: authorization review token audience and audit correlation
    case-key: 4dpfuo
    change: |
      request-path: kubectl -> api-server -> admission -> etcd
      controller-watch: desired-vs-observed
      node-path: scheduler -> kubelet -> runtime
      convergence: status-condition
    verify: kubectl version && kubectl cluster-info && kubectl get --raw /readyz?verbose
    failure: Reject client-version-only evidence, direct-etcd application access, or a claim that reconciliation guarantees application correctness.
    repair: Capture current context, server APIs, component authority, desired state, observed status, and the remaining distribution-specific boundary.
